more: v. a. S. स्तूप to heap up. 1. To pat; put on the pan. Āṭā milā to īṅdhan, chūlhā, tavā na-dārad, Roṭī pakāve kis par, ghar meṅ tavā na-dārad, Gar ṭhīkre pe thope to phir mazā na-dārad, Nau chhed, peṅdī gāyab, jis par galā na-dārad, Pānī kā garmiyoṅ meṅ jhajjar milā to aisā! On Poverty. Nazīr. If chance bring him some flour, nor fuel nor hearth he's got, Nor baking-pan—on broken shard 'twould n't taste a jot— And if in summer's heat he find a water pot, Be sure, it has nine holes, nor neck nor bottom aught, Such are the windfalls in the luckless poor man's lot! 2. To apply with the fingers or palm; to clap on; plaster. 3. To cover; hide.
v. a. 1. To support, to prop. 2. To plaster. 3. To pile, to heap.

def: s. (dakh.) A spring, font, fount, fountain ( Harris ).

adj. 1. Few; some; a little; small; scanty. 1. Rāṅḍ kā sāṅḍ, saudāgar kā ghoṛā, khāë bahut, chale thoṛā . Prov. A widow's son and a dealer's horse eat much, move slow. 2. Bahut kaṭ gaī, thoṛī rah gaī . Prov. Saying. The best part of my life is gone, but little now remains. 2. Insufficient; inadequate; low; below par; short; under; less than. Apne piyā par tan man vārūṅ, jo vārūṅ so thoṛā re . Wom. Ṭhumrī . For my beloved I'll give my body and soul, give all I can how small is still the whole? 3. Narrow; limited; confined; bounded. 4. Slight; feeble; slender; trifling. Yeh thoṛī bāt nahīṅ hai . This is no trifling matter. 5. Scarce; rare; uncommon. thoṛā bahut , adj. More or less; about; a little. thoṛā thoṛā , adv. See ahistah , 2. Thoṛā thoṛā kar-ke bahut ho jātā hai . Prov. A little and a little more makes up at last a great big store. (Every mickle makes a muckle). thoṛā thoṛā h . v. n. To feel oneself small; to be covered with shame. thoṛā sā, thoṛī sī , adj. A little; a very little. Fikr-i-maāsh, ishq-i-butāṅ, yād-i-raftagāṅ, Thoṛī sī umr meṅ koī kyā kyā kīyā kare . The care to live, the pangs of love, the grief for those who've gone, In what brief space what wondrous things by one man seen and done! thoṛā k v. a. To lessen; decrease; diminish; reduce; mitigate. thoṛe dinoṅ se , adv. For a short time; temporarily; of late; recently. thoṛā hī, thoṛaī, thoṛāī , intj. Not in the least; not a bit of it; not a jot; never. 1. Maiṅ vahāṅ thoṛe hī thā . Not a bit of me was there at all. 2. Yahāṅ merā nāl thoṛā hī gaṛā hai . Is my navel string the least bit buried here? (What is there to keep me here?). ☞ It is the practice to bury the navel string in the house. Hence an attachment to the house is believed to arise.

def: s.f. The sound made in spitting, or an imitation of that sound; — intj. (expressive of disgust, scorn, &c.) Fie!, out!, tush!, tut!
more: intj. An imitative word for the sound of hawking and spitting ( tuf ). thū thū k . v. n. 1. To spit continually. Mujhe thū thū karnā achchhā nahīṅ lagtā . 2. To spit on or at; scorn; disdain; despise. Ek laṛkā janam kā hīnā, jin dekhā, un thū thī kīnā . Riddle. Ans. Ugāldān . A spittoon. A child unborn, spat on with scorn. thū thū honā , v. n. 1. To be derided, scorned, mocked at. 2. To be disgraced, dishonored; to be come a byword. Sārī birādrī meṅ thū thū ho rahī hai. thū thū, thū thū thuddā . An expression used by boys at play when one wants to fall out. 'Hamārī thū thū hai.' 'Thū thū kare, murdā khāë .' 'I want to go out.' 'Whoe'er goes out on carrion feeds about.'
subs: v.n. To be constantly or repeatedly spitting; to spit (on or at, -par) in scorn, &c.; to flout or jeer with the exclamation thū; to deride, scorn, treat with disdain

Just enough sweetmeats to sweeten your mouth only its all one to get or not to get. 3. Thūk tak nahīṅ niglā jātā . He cannot even swallow his spittle. (His throat is so sore). thūk bilonā , v. n. Rangīn. My sides are aching, nurse, this pain I feel Not cured to-day I spit upon your skill. 2. Thūk hai tere janam meṅ! Wom.

ety: thūk˚ = Prk. थुक्क(इ) or थुक्के(इ) = S. ष्ठेव or स्थेव+कृ
def: v.n. To spit (at, -meṅ or -par), to treat with utter contempt; to vilify, abuse
more: v. a. To spit. 1. Koī ā-kar thūktā bhī nahī . Wom. No one would even spit in your house. 2. Tum thūkte ho. Ham thūkte bhī nahīṅ . You spit at me. I would n't even do that much at you. 3. Apnā ghar hag bhar, parāyā ghar thūk kā bhī ḍar . Prov. Your own house you may foul, another's you may not spit in. 2. To spit at; show the greatest contempt for one. Thūkeṅge sab log, lugāī, dekh, tere is mchṅḍe meṅ; Yeh augan tere wahīṅ chhipeṅge kisī gāoṅ aur goṅḍe meṅ . Play. Chaubolā. 3. To call names; talk about one; abuse. Muhalle ke log kyā thūkeṅge . Wom. khūn thūknā , v. n. To spit blood. thukkā fazīhatī ; Wom. tukkā fazītī . 1. Disgrace, abuse. 2. Quarrel; contention; strife.
subs: v.t. (intens. of thūknā), To spit out; to give up (in disgust, &c.), to leave, abandon
or thūk ćāṭnā, v.n. To swallow one's own words, to retract or recall a promise, to break one's word
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀣𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀇 (thukkaï), from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀣𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀢𑀺 (*thukkati). Cognate with Gujarati થૂંકવું (thū̃kvũ), Marathi थुंकणे (thuṅkṇe), Sindhi ٿُڪَڻُ (thukaṇu) / थुकणु (thukaṇu), Punjabi ਥੁੱਕਣਾ (thukkaṇā), Nepali थुक्नु (thuknu), Odia ଥୁକିବା (thukibā). By surface analysis, تُھوک (thūk, “spit; saliva”) + ـنا (-nā, verb-forming suffix).

The third day after death, when offerings made in the name of the deceased are distributed in charity. Mah. Meo marā jab jānye, jab tījā ho jāë . Prov. Know that the Mevātī is really dead when the tījā is held. ☞ It is related that a Mevātī who was indebted to a Banyā caused a report of his death to be spread abroad. His creditor, to assure himself of the fact, followed the body to the grave where he saw it interred. The friends of the reputed dead man however returned and rescued him from his living grave. The Banyā seeing him alive again gave utterance to the proverb above quoted.
s. f. The third day of a lunar fortnight.

def: s.f. A commentary, annotation, gloss

n. m. S. टीक to explain. 1. See tilak . 2. A mark made on the forehead of one setting out on a journey, pilgrimage, or great enterprise. 3. An ornamental patch of gold, silver, tinsel, etc. or a jewel worn on the centre of the forehead. Bāhar baiṭhā bannā merā ṭīkā ghaṛāve, Andar baiṭhī lāḍo merī chunnīyā lagāve . Wom. song. The bridegroom sits outside, has jewels rich made for his bride, His darling sits inside, and threads them with becoming pride. 4. A mark of distinction, chiefship, etc. Uṛad kahe 'mere māthe ṭīkā, Mujh bin byāh na hove nīkā .' Ṭesū song. The uṛad says, 'upon my brow the stamp of priesthood see, Without me, friend, your marriage rites can ne'er accomplished be.' 5. A chief; a prince; the heir apparent. 6. A stain; blot; smudge; soot. 1. Nīl kā ṭīkā, koṛh kā dāg . Prov. Of tattooing and leprosy the marks can ne'er effaced be. 2. Kālak kī koṭhṛī meṅ jo jāëgā; use ṭīkā lagegā . Prov. Who enter chambers of black paint, cant come out thence without a taint. (You cant touch pitch and not be defiled). 7. A ceremony connected with betrothal. 8. Presents in money or goods, and especially cocoanuts to the intended bridegroom from the father of the bride. 9. The ceremony of installation and investiture. 10. Inoculation; vaccination. 11. Note; annotation; comment; commentary; gloss; key. ṭīkā bhej'nā , v. a. 1. To send the ṭīkā . See group (8). 2. To betroth. ṭīkā-kartā ; A. shārah , n. m. Annotator; commentator. ṭīkā lagāne-vālā ; E. khudanyā ; Pop. fasd kholne-vālā , n. m. A vaccinator.
